architecture.summary: This is a survey and framework paper proposing 'verifier engineering' as a post-training paradigm for foundation models. The system itself is conceptual—a three-stage pipeline (Search, Verify, Feedback) that formalizes how to generate candidate outputs, evaluate them using automated verifiers, and incorporate verification signals to improve model behavior. Information flows from sampled model outputs through verifier evaluation (ranking, scoring, binary, or text feedback) into downstream training (SFT, preference learning, RL) or inference-time modifications. The paper models this as a Goal-Conditioned MDP and provides a taxonomy of existing methods across these three stages.

biblio.title: Search, Verify and Feedback: Towards Next Generation Post-training Paradigm of Foundation Models via Verifier Engineering

claims.statement: verifier engineering constitutes a fundamental pathway toward achieving Artificial General Intelligence

verifier engineering extends the construction of supervision signals beyond traditional manual feature extraction and data annotation by utilizing a suite of effective automated verifiers to perform verification tasks and provide meaningful feedback
verifier engineering shifts improvement of foundation models from a data-centric endeavor to a systematic engineering challenge emphasizing design and orchestration of complex verification systems

fine-grained verification is preferable from a machine learning perspective as it mitigates the risks of shortcut learning and bias associated with coarse-grained verification, thereby enhancing generalization

tree search significantly improves the likelihood of discovering global optimal solution, particularly in environments with complex state spaces, by simultaneously considering multiple paths and mitigating the risk of being locked into sub-optimal decisions made early on

model-based verifiers adapt to varying contexts and tasks through learning, allowing for more nuanced and context-sensitive evaluations, but introduce an element of uncertainty and can require significant training and computational resources

the central challenge in the current era is determining how to supply more effective supervision signals to foundation models to achieve general artificial intelligence capabilities, as traditional data engineering approaches have reached practical limitations

verifier engineering can be formalized as a Goal-Conditioned Markov Decision Process (GC-MDP) where search corresponds to action selection, verify maps to the reward function evaluating alignment with specified goals, and feedback relates to policy distribution optimization
